items.title: “We strongly believe that in the coming years, writing code manually won’t be a thing”: Inside the new wave of AI vibe coding tools turning prompts into plugins
items.excerpt: AI-powered plugin generators like Amorph and Pluginmaker.ai let musicians build custom audio plugins from natural-language prompts instead of traditional coding, pointing to a new 'vibe coding' workflow for music-tool creation.

items.title: Music in training sets is the new Spotify Wrapped: how genAI uses your music
items.excerpt: Producers are finding their own music inside AI training datasets after an Atlantic investigation made the databases publicly searchable, exposing widespread use of artists' work without consent or compensation.

items.title: AI Music Deals: Musicians' Note Warns to Not Override Artists' Rights
items.excerpt: A global coalition of musicians, songwriters and managers released a letter warning record labels and publishers against coercing artists into AI licensing deals, arguing creators deserve the same training-consent rights the labels demand of AI firms.

items.title: Two Notes Audio Engineering releases Genome Intro, a FREE amp and FX suite
items.excerpt: Two Notes' free Genome Intro is powered by CODEX, an AI amp playback engine that loads and plays neural amp captures from Neural Amp Modeller, AIDA-X and Proteus inside one workspace.

items.title: Apple Intros Music Understanding Framework At WWDC
items.excerpt: Apple's new on-device Music Understanding framework, unveiled at WWDC 2026, uses AI audio analysis to extract time-stamped data across six musical dimensions — rhythm, key, loudness, pace, structure and instrument activity — for app developers.

items.title: Was your music used to train AI? This free tool will tell you
items.excerpt: A free searchable database built by The Atlantic lets musicians check whether their recordings appear in four major AI music training datasets, revealing millions of tracks potentially used without explicit consent.
